ON BEHALF OF DEM PARTY GROUP TUNCER BAKIRHAN (Siirt) - Mr. President, Dear Chairman of the political parties, dear fellow MPs; I greet you all with respect and love.
Again, I send my greetings and love to our valuable people who watch us on their screens.
Again, I send my greetings and love to my friends and companions who are currently in prisons all over Türkiye, unfairly and unlawfully. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
Again, I wish God's mercy to those who lost their lives in the helicopter crash in Isparta today and offer my condolences to their families.
One in Artvin
There was a landslide. I would like to start my speech by wishing God's mercy to our citizens who lost their lives there and my condolences to their families.
Dear MPs, we are witnessing historical moments in the Middle East. The Baath regime, which has been in power in Syria for sixty-one years, collapsed as of yesterday. The Syrian people experienced great suffering, oppression and massacres during the civil war that broke out both before and after 2011. Although Syria is one of the centers of civilization in human history, it has not been able to escape oppression, cruelty, neglect, death and violence. Today there is an opportunity to enter a new era in Syria; Now is the time to act not with feelings of grudge, anger or revenge, but with the desire to create a democratic order. We want a democratic government to rule the country in Syria. The interim government to be established in Syria should make preparations for the transition to a democratic process and declare this to the world.
Syria belongs to Syrians; All powers must respect the common will of the Syrian people. The rights of all peoples and beliefs living in Syria must be guaranteed by a democratic constitution. The road to a return to a past-like political power in Syria must now be closed; Democracy is the mortar of the political solution.
Dear MPs, every bullet fired after the overthrow of the Baath regime in Syria will increase the internal chaos and turn Syria into a deeper island of war and instability. A ceasefire must be reached quickly,
The emergence of a new Lebanon or Libya, which is the address of amorphous wars, must be prevented.
The psychological and political energy that will arise after the overthrow of the sixty-one-year Baath regime will trigger new fault lines and create a great chaos on our border, which will lead to the destabilization of the Middle East and Türkiye. Attacking Manbij, while people's coexistence in the new era of Syria is being talked about, is an attempt to undermine the search for a solution in Syria and Türkiye. This is an eclipse of reason; this needs to be abandoned.
Turkey is not exempt from developments. For this reason, a new attack by the AKP government on Kurdish gains will leave stability in Syria to another spring and fuel the fire in the Middle East. On this issue, we call on the AKP government to implement the peace and solution policy towards Syria and to enter into dialogue with the north-eastern Syrian administration. All forces in the region, especially the United Nations, have a great responsibility to prevent any attacks by radical Salafi groups against Arab Alevis, Druze, Assyrians, Christians, Kurds, Armenians, Turkmens and Circassians.

The future of Syria should be built with the democratic participation of peoples and beliefs, and common will should be taken as the basis.
Dear MPs, ensuring the safe return of Syrians who are forced to live all over the world, especially in Türkiye, and who want to return to their country,
We also call on the international community and powers to ensure property, health and life security.
(Humming)
CHAIRMAN - Mr. Bakırhan, wait a second.
Dear friends, there is a very intense buzz in the hall, in the General Assembly. I request that the humming noise be reduced and removed in order to enable the speaker to speak more effectively and to be listened to more comfortably.
Here you go, Mr. Bakırhan.
TUNCER BAKIRHAN (Continuing) - Thank you.
Dear friends, we are going through a period of historical ruptures in the world and the region. In this period of historical ruptures, multiple conflicts fueling global instability are spreading rapidly. We are entering 2025 under the shadow of increasing geopolitical tensions, especially between the USA and China, global security, military competition, climate change, coups and migration mobility. Many apocalyptic trailers are beginning to appear, politically and socially. We are living in a period when global powers are helpless to find solutions against huge threats. The picture we witness in the Middle East is the summary and reality of this period.
It is possible to see the general picture in a joint article written by US and UK intelligence chiefs for an international newspaper. "The world order faces the greatest threat of war in the last forty years," both chiefs said. They said, that is, they actually admitted what was going to happen. The Ukrainian war, which changed the European security architecture and global economic balances, is in full swing.
continues. At the last NATO meeting, Russia was declared an open enemy and resources for the war were increased. Hot wars continue in many areas from Africa to Asia, from America to the Middle East, and systems and values ​​are also changing. In this period when the rulers are in a complete race to the bottom, everything is literally being reset again; It manifests itself in the form of great power competition, geoeconomic competition, digital sovereignty, cultural polarization, democracy and authoritarianism, and all countries are being drawn into a multipolar world again. Thus, a period is imagined in which parties are formed and polarization increases. As a result of all the events, far-right populism is gaining strength through cultural and political polarization, issues such as immigration, minority rights, Islamophobia, gender and identity politics are turning into management tools, and governments are being strengthened through hatred and racism. Rising war and violence have displaced 160 million people worldwide, according to the United Nations. As the struggle between democracy and authoritarianism continues to clash all over the world, authoritarian regimes define the crisis they cause as an opportunity. As a result, the place where all these events and phenomena take place is the Middle East. It is not what you say to the world, but what you do in the Middle East that is decisive.

Unfortunately, the people of the Middle East are paying the price for the pain between global and regional powers; The endless conflicts in Syria, the state of vigilance in Iran,
There is uncertainty in Iraq and in the middle of it all, Palestine and Lebanon are under Israeli attack. It is a big mistake to think that Türkiye will not be affected by this while things are changing in the Middle East. When we look at the developments globally, on the one hand, and in the Middle East, on the other hand, we should see the great storm that surrounds us and is approaching. How can we survive this fire that has the potential to affect us all? This is what politics should focus on with all its institutions and find a way. We can find this way with common sense. The state can emerge from this storm with a restorative and just transition period. This is a universal solution. We will either strengthen our social unity, democracy, rights and freedoms, or we will wait for this ring of fire to grow and come towards us.
Dear MPs, there are sharing wars in the world and great turmoil in the Middle East. The Middle East is shaken by identity-based tensions, ideological conflicts and strategic rivalries. While the hundred-year-old order is being re-established, countries with strong internal peace and strong economies directly affect the outcome. Throughout history, armies have participated in wars, but the winners are countries with strong internal peace and strong economies. Today, Türkiye is experiencing the most fragile period in terms of economic internal peace in this period where a hundred years of fractures are experienced in the Middle East. The main reason for the fragile economy is AKP's wrong policies. Social peace is becoming increasingly impossible because of the government that widens the gap between labor and capital, poor and rich, hungry and full. The poverty line exceeds 70 thousand liras
During this period, 50 million people are trying to fight poverty. On the one hand, 1.5 billion Turkish lira falls into credit follow-up in just one week, and on the other hand, there are those who earn 1.5 billion TL in a week. The dynamite laid under economic inner peace brings great decay at the end of the day, and as the system decays, social life suffers more damage. Policies of impoverishment, ignoring and denial carried out against certain segments of a people impose a refutation on the management system and the whole society, like a boomerang effect.
When we look at the 2025 budget, the share allocated from the budget for social aid and support is 651 billion Turkish lira, while the share allocated only to interest is 1 trillion 950 billion Turkish lira, which is 3 times more. Now, can we say that this budget is the budget of the poor, workers, women, labourers, civil servants and disadvantaged groups? We can't say of course. Your 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal is a compass to expand this rot. While millions of people are struggling with hunger, loan debts and foreclosures are going on, and tradesmen cannot even make a purchase, this budget you propose will deepen the problems even more. Look, in this budget proposal, an income of 12 trillion 800 billion Turkish lira is expected, the majority of which is collected from the hungry, poor and unemployed people. This resource, taken from the sweat of the poor and the laborers, is transferred to three favored groups.

1 trillion 950 billion Turkish lira for interest,
1 trillion 608 billion Turkish liras are transferred to war and security expenditures, 3 trillion Turkish liras are transferred to the capital under incentives, exceptions, exemptions and guaranteed payments. you say.
Dear MPs, it is such an order that the male-dominated system affects even the capillaries of social life. Patriarchal domination, especially with the policies of the government, imposes exploitation, poverty and massacre on women every day. The sails of the rising right-wing, male, sexist political wind in the world are mostly blown by the AKP government. Misogyny is implemented as the basic codes of power politics; Injustice and lawlessness are mostly committed against women, and poverty is experienced most deeply by women. You cannot eradicate poverty, on the contrary, you deepen it; You want people to be satisfied with the charity system you have established. Imagine, in a country, people only tell each other about discounts in supermarkets as good news.
The way to put an end to this terrible situation is to establish strong internal peace and a strong economy. The people of Türkiye do not need more interest and war policies, but a strong social peace and democracy. This is the way
It is about justice in the distribution of income and wealth, and focusing on the poor and workers instead of barons and lobbies. If this path is not taken, unfortunately, a new additional budget will have to be made in 2025. Additional budget means bringing new taxes and additional costs to the people who are already living in poverty.
Dear MPs, let us express at the beginning what we, as the DEM PARTY, will say at the end. This country is our common homeland, we can live as equal and free citizens in this common homeland, and together we can build a new Türkiye where all peoples and beliefs will live in peace and tranquility. Kurds, Arabs and Turkmens outside Türkiye's borders are relatives and compatriots of the citizens of the Republic of Türkiye, and establishing good relations with them is extremely important for regional peace in the long term. Putting cyclical power fluctuations and periodic change grounds ahead of the search for regional peace will be the greatest evil done to these lands and people in the medium and long term. I have stated before that Türkiye should be friends, not enemies, with the Kurds living outside its borders. Enmity does not make Türkiye gain, kinship does. In a process where the political equation will be re-established in Syria, dialogue with the Kurds will surely bring great benefits to Türkiye. Türkiye can be an exemplary country in the Middle East with the peaceful policies it will follow. Kurds outside Türkiye's borders are not a threat to Türkiye, they are an opportunity for peace.
The state of the Republic of Türkiye should evaluate this. Denial and exclusion cannot and should not be a policy. A regional alliance and social, economic and cultural interaction are essential to ensure peace in the Middle East. We are ready to provide all kinds of support to the steps taken by the government in this regard.

Look, in the last twenty years, the Kurds have presented a total of 12 solutions and road maps for a democratic solution: Declaration of democratic solution, dilemma of solution and non-solution in the Kurdish problem, democratic solution and peace, great peace effort, free association and peace move, peace plan, social peace and democratic participation law, road map for peace, road map and democratic liberation and free life process. These topics are suggested solution projects. These solution texts contain prescriptions for all today's crises. All of them are available in the state archives. Unfortunately, these solution opportunities were not evaluated or even taken into account. On the contrary, suppression reports were prepared that led the Kurdish issue to deadlock. Let us remind you of some of the reports prepared so far: Abdülhalik Renda Report, Cemil Uybadın Report, Hamdi Bey Report, Ali Cemal Bardakçı Report, Eastern Reform Plan, Şükrü Kaya Report, Hüseyin Alpdoğan Report and dozens of other reports that I cannot name were written to deny the Kurdish problem. In other words, while the Kurds are preparing road maps and reports for a solution, unfortunately, reports are being prepared that deny the Kurds.
Well, I want to ask: Which of these reports was successful? Did these reports serve any purpose other than exacerbating the problem? We all live and witness together. Look, in this country's memory there is also a search for solutions other than denial. It is necessary to support these search for solutions. The state contacted through Özal in 93, Prime Minister Necmettin Erbakan established contact in 96, the General Staff established direct contact in 97, the General Staff established face-to-face contact with the state in 99, the military wing held continuous meetings between 2000 and 2005, and the executive branch held meetings after 2005, mainly in 2010. The process that took place between 2013 and 2015 reached a historical point like the Dolmabahçe agreement. In other words, dozens of solution opportunities have arisen since '93. Unfortunately, these opportunities could not evolve into peace, and unfortunately, whenever the opportunity for peace was missed, denial continued to be the official language of the state. Look, let me tell you a historical anecdote: Justice Party Edirne Deputy İlhami Ertem, who took the floor in this Parliament in 1964, said, "No government in Türkiye has made a distinction between east and west." When he says, retired MP Mustafa Remzi Bucak writes a letter to him and says: "You can deceive a few naive people, but never history." Friends who came out and spoke a while ago also said that there is no similar distinction, but unfortunately neither us nor
You can never cheat history. We still hear from these platforms the same words that were said sixty years ago. Well, "None." Did you say that the problem was solved? "There is no distinction." Did this solve the problem? The Republic has been skidding on the Kurdish issue for a hundred years, the whole world evolved into a completely different place a hundred years ago, but this mind has not made any progress in a hundred years. Kurds have changed and transformed a lot in a hundred years; So why don't you change one inch? Again, there is a very simple question: Are you in favor of a peaceful, democratic solution to the Kurdish problem or not? The people of Türkiye are really curious about this.
It is a famous story: When Cairo Radio first started broadcasting in Kurdish in the 1950s, the ambassador hurriedly went to the high officials and said, "You cannot broadcast in Kurdish." says.

Thereupon, the officials asked the ambassador: "Are there Kurds in Türkiye?" To the question, the ambassador replied: "None." "If there are no Kurds, then what are you making a problem?" He answers with a smile. Thereupon, the ambassador leaves the conversation quietly. Denying the Kurdish problem and denying the rights of the Kurds will only make you look ridiculous in this age. Denying this people, who are known to the people of the world, who are the symbol of light against the darkness of ISIS, and with whom you have lived around a common destiny for a thousand years, will put you in ridiculous situations that you cannot describe. We said, "Let this issue be resolved through democratic means and let's turn from this historical mistake." The more we say, the more we are threatened with arrest; "Dialogue." As we say, all our employees are arrested; We take over the municipality, it is forcibly confiscated; "Respect for the elect."
We say, the honor and will of millions are being manipulated through trustees. Unfortunately, we are faced with such a reality.
Dear MPs, imagine a Kurd with a hundred-year-old memory, who spent his life in violence, hunger and migration, and who lost his relatives; Think of a Turk who lost his child during the conflict. If this Kurd or this Turk still wants peace, what we need to do is not to hit the hand of peace extended by these people with a stick; What you need to do is "Despite all this pain, he wants peace." It means bowing respectfully in front of these people and doing what is necessary. We are here with all our life, our memory, our tradition of struggle; we are for peace. As the Zapatistas say, even if our feet sink into the mud of history, our heads look towards a bright future. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
Dear MPs, we stated that we see the discussions initiated by Mr. Bahçeli since October 1 as positive and important. "On this issue, our hands are open for the peace of Türkiye." We said, "We, as the DEM PARTY, are ready to fulfill our responsibility in this regard." we said. The majority of the opposition parties have demonstrated a very determined will for a democratic solution and peace. Maybe for the first time we are witnessing a great collaboration; This is a very valuable attitude, a historical opportunity; this opportunity
Let's not waste it. Türkiye's experiences in the outside world, especially in the Moro Philippines, and its search for peace internally since 1993 have created a comprehensive peace corpus. Again, both the Kurdish reports of the 1990s and the current attitude of the main opposition party are very important. The main opposition also has an important solution memory. In fact, with this accumulation, a body of knowledge regarding the solution of the Kurdish issue has been formed, a diagnosis has been made and a prescription has been written; now is the time for peace. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
Dear MPs, we need to initiate the peace and brotherhood project by drawing the Turkish-Kurdish alliance on a democratic basis against the imperialists' policy of pitting people against each other in our region. Today, Rojava is the testing ground of the historical Turkish-Kurdish alliance. The mentality that sees the Kurdish gains in Rojava as an enemy to itself will cause the greatest damage to this historical alliance. Let's rebuild the historical unity from Qamishli, Kobani to Ankara on an equal and fair basis. The government has great responsibilities in this regard. In particular, President Erdoğan has a constructive responsibility.

Mr. Erdoğan, the Kurdish issue is Türkiye's most historical issue awaiting a solution. The opportunity to make history by solving this issue is waiting for you. I would like to express once again before the Parliament that, as the DEM PARTY, we will do our part on this issue. This Parliament came up with a solution
should be mentioned and Ankara should present its solution without turning its face elsewhere. If there is a vision for Ankara, there must also be an Ankara solution. If we are sincere in our search for a real and radical solution, we must find this solution not outside but in the common past of Turks and Kurds and their determination to build the future together. Consolidating power from the capitals of other countries should be avoided; seeking opportunism in this critical period will not offer anyone a permanent solution.
Dear MPs, in the 11th century, the 16th century and the 20th century, Turks and Kurds made alliances and acted together at the most critical junctions of history. Together, we can carry the thousand-year-old historical alliance since the Battle of Manzikert to new centuries. Let's carry the historical alliance from the spirit of Manzikert to the spirit of Eşme to new centuries together. For a hundred years, we have lived a painful history in the dilemma of rebellion and suppression; People died, they were forced to migrate, villages were burned and destroyed, and economic resources were used to bring trouble instead of healing. The idea of ​​common destiny, which has won for millennia, has been met with a denial that has made us lose for a hundred years. We have been going back for a hundred years, we have gained nothing, but we have lost lives and economic resources. We must take quick steps to close this century-old parenthesis and get rid of the dilemma of rebellion and suppression. Let's base the Turkish-Kurdish alliance on "win-win" politics and strengthen our commonality of destiny. between Turks and Kurds
A thousand years of togetherness is not the result of a coincidence, but of a common destiny. This unity is not the product of obligation, but of voluntary solidarity and a historical alliance. Today, in the light of these deep-rooted ties, we have to go beyond periodic opportunism and paranoia and build the future together. Today, the ruling mind is still making fruitless efforts to eliminate border security paranoia.
Look, let's take you back five centuries: Suleiman the Magnificent stated that he saw living together with the Kurds as the security of both his borders and his empire. The recipe drawn up five centuries ago is still valid today. Today, the security of Turks depends on establishing an equal and democratic life with Kurds. The basis of protection from regional chaos is a Turkish-Kurdish partnership updated with equality and democracy.
I would like to underline that those who see the Kurds as old Kurds and who yearn to rule over them are making a mistake. This misconception is a defeat for everyone. Insisting on such a mistake will cause great harm to the historical Turkish-Kurdish alliance. The Republic of Türkiye should be a state to which Kurds, like all citizens living in this country, feel like they belong. Our expectation from the state is to be a democratic and inclusive state that embraces all citizens without discrimination and accepts their differences.
(Humming)
CHAIRMAN - Mr. Bakırhan, wait a second.

Dear friends, I really don't know.
Dear friends, there is an unbearable humming sound here, that is, the sound is increasing until here. I beg you,
Do not be unfair to the orator, do not be unfair to those who listen to the orator, do not be unfair to the General Assembly; It would probably be more beneficial if we listened a little more calmly.
Here you go.
TUNCER BAKIRHAN (Continuing) - Voices intervened at a very important place, I will repeat it if my friends want to listen.
The Republic of Türkiye should be a state to which Kurds, like all citizens living in this country, feel like they belong. Our expectation from the state is to be a democratic and inclusive state that embraces all citizens without discrimination and accepts their differences. This is how a real and democratic Türkiye is built, and the identity of true Turkishness is created around these values. Learning from past mistakes and not dwelling on those mistakes is not a sign of weakness, but a sign of true maturity. This approach not only deepens our historical alliance, but also creates a solid basis for strengthening peace and building a lasting reconciliation. Here is a statement that offers insurance on this solid ground, came from İmralı. Mr. Abdullah Öcalan "Isolation continues." He said, but then added, "If the conditions arise, I have the theoretical and practical power to move this process from the ground of conflict and violence to the legal and political ground." he said. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
We ask the government: Do you have the theoretical and practical power to find a solution? All of Türkiye has been waiting for this question with curiosity for days. If your aim is to solve the Kurdish issue and you point to Öcalan as the address for this - this is the right choice - then why do you continue to keep the doors of İmralı closed?
Why are you isolating peace? Insisting on peace, growing social solidarity and building our future on the basis of brotherhood is the best way to both make sense of today and save tomorrow. We can establish a country where all citizens of Türkiye will live in peace and brotherhood with a democratic constitution, and we can enter the 2nd century with a constitution that covers everyone.
Before ending my speech, I would like to make an important call: We can make a new start in 2025, in the 103rd year of the republic. This Assembly has the chance to undertake the founding of the democratic republic. Let this Parliament have the honor of creating a country where 85 million people will feel like they belong. It is time to hear the voice of peace that fills the sky and the earth, for which these lands have been longing for a hundred years, and it is time to bring the peace hanging on the horizon to these lands.
With these feelings, I send my greetings, respect and love to all of you and all our people who watch us on their screens. (Standing applause from DEM PARTY benches; applause from CHP and MHP benches)

DEM PARTİ GRUBU ADINA TUNCER BAKIRHAN (Siirt) - Sayın Başkan, partilerin Kıymetli Genel Başkanları, çok değerli milletvekili arkadaşlar; hepinizi saygıyla sevgiyle selamlıyorum.
Yine, cezaevinde bulunan, başta Figen Yüksekdağ ve Selahattin Demirtaş şahsında bütün arkadaşlarımıza Meclisten selam ve sevgilerimizi yolluyoruz. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışalar)
Sevgili Sırrı Süreyya Önder şahsında da yıllardır bizimle birlikte barış ve demokrasi mücadelesi veren ama bugün aramızda olmayan bütün arkadaşlarımızı da rahmet ve minnetle anıyorum. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)
Yine, bugün, İstanbul'da bir uyuşturucu baskınında yaşamını yitiren Polis Memuru Emre Albayrak'a
Allah'tan rahmet, ailesine başsağlığı dileklerimi ileterek konuşmama başlamak istiyorum.
Değerli milletvekilleri, bugün burada yalnızca 2026 yılına ait gelir ve gider kalemlerini konuşmuyoruz. Bütçe sıradan bir belge değildir, bir ülkenin gerçek aynasıdır. Bu aynada ahlaktan hukuka, ekonomiden sosyolojiye kadar her şey çok net görünür. Servetin kimde toplanacağından kimlerin yoksul kalacağına, kimin hukuk içinde sayılacağına kadar geniş bir yelpazeyi iktidarın tercihleri belirliyor. Tek bir örnek vereceğim: Toplumsal barışı konuştuğumuz bugünlerde önümüzde duran 2026 bütçesinde savunma ve güvenlik kalemlerine ayrılan kaynak önceki yıla oranla yüzde 34 oranında artırılmış durumda. Savunma ve güvenlik harcamaları artırılırken sosyal devletin, adaletin, yerel demokrasinin, barışın ve hukukun bütçesi kısılıyorsa orada bir tercih konuşuyoruz demektir. Türkiye her devlet gibi savunma ve güvenlik için tabii ki bütçe ayıracak ama savunmaya 10 verilip sağlık, eğitim ve adalete 1 bırakılıyorsa bu denge sorunu değil bir tercih sorunudur. İşte, tam da bu yüzden diyoruz ki: Bütçe, rakamları değil siyasi tercihleri konuşmaktır. Türkiye tarihi boyunca yaşanan bazı tarihsel kırılmalar siyasi, ekonomik ve toplumsal hayatımızı baş aşağı götürdü. Takvimler 4 Mart 1925'i gösterdiğinde
cumhuriyet yönetimi Takrir-i Sükûn'da değil demokratikleşme rotasında ısrar etseydi Türkiye'nin harcı demokrasiyle karılırdı. 1960'lı yıllarda demokrasinin özü olan millî iradeye saygı esas alınsaydı Başbakanını ve bakanlarını idam eden bir ülke utancını hafızalarımızda taşımayacaktık. 1970'li yıllar demokratik olgunluk içerisinde geçseydi ne gencecik insanlarımız idama giderdi ne 24 Ocak Kararlarıyla emekçiler hedef alınır ne de 12 Eylülde tank paletleri siyasetçileri ve halkı ezebilirdi. 1992'de bir halkın bayramı olan "Nevroz" tehdit olarak değil ortak değer olarak kabul edilseydi karanlık yapılar 90'lı yıllarımıza karabasan gibi çökmeyebilirdi. 2007 yılında bu ülkede vesayetçilerin değil demokratik normların sözü geçerli olsaydı "367" adlı darbe girişimi tarihimizin kara sayfalarında yer almazdı. Tarih 15 Temmuz darbe girişimini yazdığında cevabımız 20 Temmuz OHAL ilanı değil de daha fazla demokrasi, adalet ve hukuk olsaydı bu ülke Orta Doğu'nun tüm halklarına büyük bir umut olacaktı. Bugün riskleri ve tehditleri değil umutları, emekçilerin, ezilenlerin bütçesini konuşuyor olacaktık. Takvimler 4 Kasım 2016'ya geldiğinde Selahattinler, Figenler evlerinden alınmasaydı, belediyelere kayyım atanmasaydı demokratik siyaset darbelenmezdi. 2025'ten objektifleri
geçmişin acı hatıralarına çevirdiğimizde, antidemokratik her uygulama bu ülkenin yurttaşlarına yoksulluk, gözyaşı, acı ve keder olarak döndü. Türkiye'de cumhuriyet demokrasiyle buluşsaydı bu topraklarda yaşayan her yurttaşın bambaşka bir hikâyesi olabilirdi.
Değerli Başkan, saygıdeğer milletvekilleri; bir tarihsel kırılma var ki ülkemizin bütün enerjisini harcadı; devasa toplumsal ve ekonomik maliyetlere neden oldu. 1923 sonrası başlayan Kürt isyanları ve bu isyanlar karşısında gelişen bastırmalarla bir yüzyılı geride bıraktık. Kürt meselesini konuşurken önce şunu tespit etmemiz gerekiyor: Bu ülkeyi bir asırdır yönetenlerin en temel hatası Kürt meselesini yanlış teşhis etmesidir. Teşhisi yanlış olunca Kürt sorununu ortadan kaldırma yolları da hep hatalı oldu. Kürt meselesinde yıllardır hep düğüm üstüne düğüm atılıyor, bu düğüm âdeta Gordion düğümüne dönüştü. Yıllar boyunca bu mesele terör parantezine sıkıştırıldı, "Geri kalmışlık." denildi, "Kandırılmış, aldatılmış bir avuç insan." denildi. Oysa Kürt meselesi az gelişmişlik sorunu değildir, Kürt meselesi kandırılmış ya da aldatılmış bir topluluğun problemi de değildir, Kürt meselesi terör sorunu hiç değildir; eşit yurttaşlık meselesidir, demokratik haklar meselesidir, bir varlık meselesidir ama en önemlisi Kürt meselesi bir hukuk meselesidir. Kürt'ün hukukunu tanıma üniter devlet için de pekâlâ mümkündür. "Üniter devlet" demek ne devletin inkârı
ne de Kürt'ün inkârıdır. Kürtlerin eşit yurttaşlar olarak hakkını savunması üniter devlet için bir risk değil, aksine bir güvencedir. Kürt meselesi günlük siyasetin gürültüsüne kurban edilecek bir başlık değil, canların yitirildiği, ocakların söndüğü tarihsel bir olgudur. Kürt meselesi yıllarca düğüm aklıyla yönetildi ve sonuç hep hüsran oldu. Artık barış siyasetini çözüm aklıyla kuralım diyoruz.
Saygıdeğer milletvekilleri, tarihe hangi ufuktan bakarsanız yol haritanız da ona göre şekillenir. Devletin baktığı tarih ufkunda bazı hakikatler maalesef görmezden gelindi. 1921 Anayasası'nı hazırlayan komisyonun sözcüsü İsmail Suphi Soysallıoğlu Birinci Meclis tutanaklarında yerelliği önceleyen adımlar öneriyordu. Benzer şekilde Mustafa Kemal meselesinde de hakkaniyetin terazisini kurmak gerekiyor. 1920, 1921, 1922'deki Mustafa Kemal tarihin tozlu raflarına gönderilirken 1923 sonrasının Atatürk'ü resmî anlatının başköşesinde tutuluyor. 1921 Anayasası'nı kaleme alırken yerel demokrasiyi kabul eden Mustafa Kemal'i tarihten silmemek gerek, tarihin tozlu raflarından indirmek gerek. Bu hakikati silen resmî anlatıyı artık bir tarafa bırakmalıyız, gerçeğe odaklanmalıyız. 1921'in yerelliği önceleyen o kurucu ufku bugün unutulmuş bir imkân olarak karşımızda duruyor. Tam yüz altı yıl önce Erzurum Kongresi'nin sonuç bildirgesinde Türk-Kürt ortaklığına atfen şunlar yazılır:
"Saadet ve felakette tam ortaklığı kabul eder ve gelecek hakkında aynı amacı hedef alır." Yani daha kuruluş aşamasında ortak vatan fikri ile hukuk fikri yan yana duruyor, sonra hukuk kısmı unutuluyor. Cumhuriyet, Kürtlerin omuz verdiği bir kurtuluşun ardından ilan ediliyor ama Kürt'ün hukuku kurucu metinlerin dışına itiliyor. Sayın Devlet Bahçeli 18 Kasım 2025 tarihli grup konuşmasında cumhuriyetin kuruluş dönemine dair çok önemli bir tespitte bulundu, "Hazırlık ve mayalanma dönemi kongreler marifetiyle yani demokratik yollarla icra edilmiştir." demesi aslında bugün daha fazla demokrasiyi esas almayı işaret ediyor. Nasıl ki o gün Osmanlı İmparatorluğu'nun derin bunalımı kongreler ve Meclis eliyle yani halkın iradesinin çoğulcu temsil biçimiyle aşılabildi ise bugün Türkiye'nin karşı karşıya olduğu bölgesel tehditlere ve içerideki çoklu krizlere çözüm de ancak demokrasiyle mümkün olacaktır. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)
Tarihe dürüstçe baktığımızda Balkanlarda yaşanan ağır yenilginin önemli sebeplerinden birinin Anadolu'da sağlanamayan birlik olduğunu görürüz. Kürt tehlikede ise Türk de tehlikededir. Kaderleri iç içe geçen iki halkız. Millî Mücadele'nin kazanılmasının sırrı ise Anadolu'da sağlanan birlikteliktir. 1990'ların başında Sovyetler Birliği çökerken Türkiye için çok büyük jeopolitik fırsatlar doğdu aslında ancak
Kürt meselesini çözmemekte ısrar eden anlayış tüm enerjiyi içeri harcadı. Devletin etrafını saran çeteler, karanlık yapılar bu fırsatları heba etti. Kürt meselesi çözülebilseydi 90'lar Türkiye için muazzam bir demokrasi ve sıçrama dönemine dönüşebilirdi. Bir yüzyılı iç gerilimlerle geçirdik, artık geçmişe bakıp ders almak zorundayız. İstiklal Marşı şairi Mehmet Akif Ersoy "Tarihi 'tekerrür' diye tarif ediyorlar/Hiç ibret alınsaydı tekerrür mü ederdi?" der. Biz tarihten doğru dersi çıkarmaya, "vifakı kadime" yani sonsuz ve kadim kardeşliğe davet ediyoruz herkesi. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Tehlikeleri birlikte göğüsleyeceğimiz ama saadeti de ortak yaşayacağımız yeni yüzyıla artık hep birlikte adım atmalıyız.
Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri; yüzyılın başında, cihan devletinden geriye Anadolu kaldı. Bu devasa küçülme, Türklerde bir türlü dinmeyen tarihsel korkuların tohumunu ekti. Kürtlerde ise yarı yolda bırakılmışlığın güvensizlik ve kuşkusu kaldı ama bir hakikat daha var; birbirimizden başka çaremiz yok, bizler bir evin içindeki odanın birbirine bakan iki duvarıyız, iç barışı besleyecek güçlü bir hafızaya sahibiz. Bu coğrafya yüzyıllarca sadece devletlerin, imparatorlukların değil hikmetin, merhametin, adaletin de doğduğu, yaşadığı, yayıldığı bir coğrafyadır. Bu yüzden değil mi ki bir Mevlevî ilahisi, bir Türk bozlağı,
bir Kürt "..."(*), bir Ermeni ağıdı, bir Laz tulumu duyunca aynı duygunun paydaşları olmamız. "..."(*) ozanlar bu kadim kültürden beslendiler, arifler de dervişler de aynı gözeden su içtiler. Kürt şair Cigerxwin "..."(*) dizesinde her zenginliği ırmakların çağıldaması olarak tarif eder. İşte bizim de duygumuz Cigerxwin'un duygusudur. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Büyük edebiyatçı Yaşar Kemal "Bir kuşun iki kanadıyız, biri kırılırsa uçamayız." diyerek birimizin diğeri olmadan kendini bulamayacağını ifade etmiştir. Barışa ve kardeşliğe giden yol Yunus Emre'nin sözlerinde saklı. Ne diyor Yunus: "Gelin tanış olalım. İşi kolay kılalım." Barışı ve beraberliği büyütmek tanışmaktan, diyalogdan geçer; konuşmak ve komşu kelimelerinin konmak yani bir yere yerleşmek fiilinden türediğini biliyoruz. Konuşmak ve komşuluk Kürtler ve Türkler için aynı dalda durmak, aynı manzaraya birlikte bakmaktır. Bunu unuttuğumuzda ayrılıklar, hatırladığımızda birliktelikler büyür.
Değerli milletvekilleri, yarım asırdır süren çatışmalı süreçte büyük acı ve yıkımlara tanık olduk. Kaybeden milyonlar oldu ama ne yazık ki kazananlar da oldu. Çatışmadan beslenen bir sermaye ve bürokrasi kesimi, düşmanlık siyasetinden beslenen bazı siyasi odaklar da oldu. Bu azınlık grup devletin, siyasetin, medyanın, ekonominin
her yerine sızdı. İşte, "darbe mekaniği" dediğimiz şey tam da bu her yere sızan dinamikler aracılığıyla oluyor. Sayın Öcalan'ın "darbe mekaniği" ifadesi çok çarpıtıldı. Darbe mekaniği birbirinden ayrıksı görünen birçok çevrenin tarihsel korku ve refleksleri örgütleyerek harekete geçirmesidir. Darbe mekaniğinin üstesinden barış iradesini, çözüm kararlılığını güçlendirerek ancak gelebiliriz. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) İtalyan düşünür Gramsci der ki: "Eski dünya ölüyor, yenisi doğamıyor. Bu alaca karanlıkta canavarlar türüyor." Bugün içinde bulunduğumuz eşik tam da budur. Bugün barışın doğuşunu engelleyenler yani kendi çıkarını ortak iyiliğin önüne koyanlara karşı her zamankinden daha fazla kararlı olmalıyız, yeni bir günü hep birlikte doğurabilmeliyiz.
Ekranları başında bizi izleyen değerli halklarımız, bugün iktidarın adil ve demokratik bir yönetim anlayışına geçmesi asıl meseledir. 86 milyon yurttaşı, 100 binlerce okulu, binlerce kamu kurumunu, milyonlarca insanı merkezden yönetmeye çalışmak artık hem imkânsızdır hem de sağlıksızdır. Demokratik aklın gereği yerel yönetimlerin güçlendirilmesidir, zamanın ruhu yerel demokrasidir. Mahalledeki sorunu Ankara'da tek bir masadan çözemezsiniz. Hukuku ve demokrasiyi rota edinemeyen bir Türkiye ne idari ne etnik ne de inançsal gerilimleri çözebilir. Dicle kıyısındaki, Hemşin Yaylası'ndaki bir köyün derdini ancak o köyle birlikte karar veren
bir yerellik çözebilir. Yerel demokrasi ülkeyi bir arada tutan harçtır. Yerel yönetimlerin kayyum uygulamalarıyla değil, halkın seçtiği temsilciler aracılığıyla yönetilmesini istiyoruz. Yerel yönetimlerin güçlendirilmesi Diyarbakırlı yurttaşın olduğu kadar Manisalı yurttaşın da hakkıdır. Bu adımlar, sadece Kürtlerin hukukunu değil, cumhuriyetin de hukuk içinde meşruiyetini pekiştirecek önemli adımlardır.
Değerli arkadaşlar, Kürt meselesinin son kırk yılı konuşulurken herkesin bildiği ama çoğu zaman yüksek sesle tartışmaktan kaçındığı bir gerçek var: Sayın Öcalan bu meselenin çözümünde yok sayılması mümkün olmayan bir aktördür. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) 1993'ten bu yana farklı dönemlerde çeşitli ateşkes ve müzakere çağrıları yaptığını hepimiz biliyoruz. 1 Ekim 2024'te Meclis açılışı vesilesiyle başlayan çözüm tartışmaları bir kez daha bize şunu gösterdi: Bu mesele susturarak değil konuşarak çözülür. Çatışmanın sona ermesi yönünde etkisi olabilecek her aktör, hukuk çerçevesinde, şeffaf ve demokratik bir müzakere zemini içinde bulunmalıdır. Bu sürecin seyrini belirlemiş Öcalan'ın barış için söz kurması kadar doğal bir şey yoktur. Bunun yolu da yaşam, iletişim ve çalışma şartlarının sağlanmasındadır. Tam da bu noktada, Sayın Öcalan'ın "Kürtleri cumhuriyete hukuk kapısından dâhil etmek istiyoruz." tespitinin çok önemli ve kritik olduğunu belirtmeliyiz. Cumhuriyet sadece
1923'te ilan edilmiş bir rejim değişikliği değildir, cumhuriyet ilan edilmiş ama eksik bırakılmıştır. Bugün artık o eksikliği tamamlamak zorundayız, bunu Kürt olgusunu cumhuriyetin yasallığına ekleyerek tamamlayabiliriz. Peki, sadece Kürtleri dâhil ederek bu eksiklik tamamlanır mı? Tabii ki hayır. Cumhuriyetimizin 2'nci yüzyılında, dışlanmış kesimlerin de merkeze geldiği yeni ve demokratik bir Türkiye'yi arzuluyoruz. Cumhuriyetin 2'nci yüzyılı demokrasiyle taçlandığında bütün kesimlerin ülkeye aidiyet duygusu ve bağlılığı artar.
Peki, ülkenin iç bütünlüğünü sağlamak için neler yapabiliriz? Anayasa'da eşit yurttaşlık ilkesini güçlendiren kimliklerüstü bir hukuk dili kurabiliriz, ana dilde eğitim ve kamusal hizmetlere erişimi evrensel hak olarak güvence altına alabiliriz. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Demokratik entegrasyon yasaları çıkararak karşılıklı güven ve kararlılıkla toplumsal uyumu güçlendirebiliriz. Barış yasasıyla toplumdaki etnik, inançsal, sınıfsal gerilimleri eşitlik ve özgürlük temelinde çözebiliriz. AİHM ve AYM kararlarının eksiksiz uygulanmasını sağlayabilir, ceza ve infaz mevzuatını demokratik standartlara çekebiliriz.
Bugünü konuşurken tarihin kapısını aralayıp geçmişe bir bakalım. 1500'lü yıllarda Osmanlı hükümdarı Kürtlere bir notla birlikte altında mührü bulunan beyaz bir kâğıt gönderir. Notta aynen şöyle yazar: "Dilediğinizi yazın, kanun değerindedir."
Bu anlatı bir yönetim aklının Kürtleri ne kadar iyi tanıdığını ve kıymet biçtiğini göstermesi açısından çok önemlidir. Bugün bizim ihtiyacımız beyaz kâğıdı sadece Kürtlere değil, tüm yurttaşlara uzatan "Gelin, bu ülkenin hukukunu birlikte oluşturalım." diyen demokratik cumhuriyet aklıdır. Artık yüz yıllık cumhuriyete eşlik eden ikili hukuk anlayışına son verip bütüncül hukukla barışı kurmak gerekiyor.
Değerli arkadaşlar, yüz yıl önce emperyalist güçler bu coğrafyayı cetvelle böldüler. Bugün Orta Doğu'da âdeta bitimsiz hâle gelen gerilimlerin, soykırımların, göçlerin, isyanların temel nedeni emperyalist güçlerin yüz yıl önceki müdahaleleridir; sadece sınırları değil kaderlerimizi de çizdiler. Artık bu girdaptan çıkmalıyız, Orta Doğu'da sınırları değiştirmeden anlamsızlaştırmalıyız. Toplumsal etkileşimi büyütecek, ekonomik entegrasyon ve siyasi istikrarı sağlayarak bölge barışının anahtarını ellerimize alabiliriz çünkü bu bölge halkları savaşmaktan yoruldu; Türkler, Kürtler, Araplar ve Farslar artık refah ve karşılıklı saygı içinde birlikte yaşamak istiyor. Bölge halkları cepheden cepheye sürülmekten, kayıpların mateminden yorgun düştü. Filistin'de yaşanan trajedi bu yorgunluğun vicdanlara kazınan en derin yarasıdır. Filistin halkının yaşadığı zulüm artık sonsuza dek bitmelidir. Sykes-Picot'u tarihin çöp sepetine atmanın yegâne yolu bölgede birleştirici politikalar uygulamaktan geçer. Tam da burada, Orta Doğu'nun
kanayan ülkesi hâline gelen Suriye'de de ayrıştırıcı modeller yerine birleştirici modellere katkı sunmalıyız. Suriye'den öğrenilecek en büyük ders, askerî seçeneklerin değil diyaloğun çözüm olduğudur. Suriye'de yerel yönetimlerin güçlü olduğu, farklılıklara saygı gösteren ademimerkeziyetçi bir yönetim anlayışının en uygun çözüm olduğuna inanıyoruz. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Oradaki bütün halklar bizim kardeşlerimizdir. Türkiye, oradaki bütün halklar için güvenilir bir ortak olmalıdır. Demokratik Orta Doğu birliği, halkların ve inançların ortak iradesiyle inşa edilebilir. Türkiye, cumhuriyet birikimi, coğrafi konumu ve Kürt-Türk ortak tarihî mirasıyla bu süreçte öncü bir rol oynayabilir. Türkiye, Kürtlerle birlikte düşünen bir bölge stratejisi kurarsa kazançlı çıkar. Bu anlamda, Kuzey ve Doğu Suriye'ye güvenlik merceğinden değil demokratik bir diyalog penceresinden bakabilmeliyiz. Elbette böyle tarihî bir dönüşüm tüm siyasi aktörlerin katkısını ve cesaretini gerektiriyor. Bu yüzden buradan tüm kesimlere samimi bir çağrı yapmak istiyorum, öncelikle yürütme erkine sesleniyorum: İktidardaki güç olarak en büyük sorumluluk sizin omuzlarınızdadır. Devleti kurtarmak sadece savaşta değil barışta dirayetle de olur. Bugün, yüzlerce yıllık tarihe geçecek eşiğin tam ağzındasınız. Lütfen, biraz daha kararlılık, samimiyet ve cesaret gösterin. Güvenlikçi çizgide ısrar edenlere de sesleniyorum: Her kavga elbette bir gün sulhla biter.
Her çatışma nihayetinde konuşarak, diyalogla ve müzakereyle biter; silahlı kuvvetlerin en deneyimli kişileri bile "Bu iş silahla bitmez." demektedirler. Sizlerden beklenen, barış çabalarına engel olmak değil, tam tersine, kolaylaştırıcı olmaktır. Çatışmasızlık herkesin kazandığı bir atmosferdir. Barıştan daha büyük bir ulusal güvenlik olabilir mi? Ana muhalefet çevrelerine de sesleniyorum: İlkesel bir duruşla barışı bu ülkeye armağan etmek zorundayız, bundan daha kutsal bir siyasal görev önümüzde yok.
Kürt meselesinde çözümden yana olmayan, çözümü başka bahara erteleyen bir muhalefet Türkiye'ye alternatif olamaz, bu sorunun çözümü sadece devlete ve iktidara bırakılamaz. Merkez sağ, kararsız ve milliyetçi kardeşlerime de sesleniyorum: Bu ülkede hiçbir halkı ötekileştirmeden, onlara da hakkını teslim ederek birliği sağlamak mümkündür. Zora dayalı birliktelik değil rızaya dayalı birlikteliği kurmaktan sizler de sorumlusunuz, "Önce diyalog, önce halk." diyebilmelisiniz. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Türkiye'nin tüm renklerine sesleniyorum: Gelin, önyargıları kaldıralım; eşitlik temelinde, kardeşlik hukukunu canlandırarak ülkeyi güçlendirelim, demokratikleştirelim diyorum. Türkiye hepimizin ülkesidir. Devletin geçmişte yapılan hatalarla yüzleşmesi ve daha kapsayıcı ve kuşatıcı bir siyaset uygulaması için de DEM PARTİ olarak elimizden geleni yapacağımızı bir kez daha buradan belirtmek istiyorum. Biz, demokratik haklar ve özgürlükleri herkes için istiyoruz, demokratik alanın
genişletilmesi için herkesle bir araya gelmeye hazırız. Bu ülke insanı aslında çok şey istemiyor; kendi kimliği ve inancıyla özgürce soluk aldığı, yoksulluğun olmadığı, şafak vakti çalınan kapının korkuyu değil güveni çağırdığı bir Türkiye'de yaşamak istiyor. Bu kürsüden Meclisi korkunun ataleti yerine cesaretin aklına, hesabi aklın dar terazisi değil hasbiliğin geniş ufkuna çağırıyor ve davet ediyorum. Barışı, cesaretin harcıyla ve 86 milyonun iradesiyle kuracağız. Demokratik Türkiye'nin taşıyıcı ve inşa edici gücü olmaya hazırız. Son grup konuşmasında Cumhurbaşkanı Sayın Erdoğan "Bu sefer başaracağız." dedi. Biz de buradan açıkça ifade ediyoruz: Evet, bu sefer başarmak istiyoruz, başaracağız.
Hepinize saygılarımı ve şükranlarımı sunuyorum. (DEM PARTİ, AK PARTİ, MHP ve YENİ YOL sıralarından alkışlar)

ON BEHALF OF DEM PARTY GROUP TUNCER BAKIRHAN (Siirt) - Mr. President, esteemed Chairman of the parties, dear fellow MPs; I greet you all with respect and love.
Again, we send our greetings and love from the Parliament to all our friends in prison, especially Figen Yüksekdağ and Selahattin Demirtaş. (Applause from the DEM PARTY benches)
I remember with mercy and gratitude all our friends, in the person of dear Sırrı Süreyya Önder, who have been fighting for peace and democracy with us for years but are not with us today. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
Again, today, to Police Officer Emre Albayrak, who lost his life in a drug raid in Istanbul.
I would like to start my speech by expressing my condolences to his family and may God have mercy on him.
Dear MPs, today we are not just talking about income and expense items for 2026. The budget is not an ordinary document, it is the true mirror of a country. Everything, from morality to law, from economics to sociology, appears very clearly in this mirror. The preferences of the government determine a wide range of issues, from who will collect the wealth to who will remain poor and who will be considered within the law. I will give just one example: As we talk about social peace, the resources allocated to defense and security items in the 2026 budget have been increased by 34 percent compared to the previous year. If the budget of the social state, justice, local democracy, peace and law is reduced while defense and security expenditures are increased, then we are talking about a choice there. Türkiye, like every state, will of course allocate a budget for defense and security, but if 10 is given to defense and 1 is left for health, education and justice, this is not a balance problem but a choice problem. That's exactly why we say: Budget is about talking about political choices, not numbers. Some historical ruptures throughout Türkiye's history turned our political, economic and social life upside down. When the calendar shows March 4, 1925
If the republican administration had insisted on the democratization route rather than Takrir-i Sükûn, Türkiye would have been mixed with democracy. If respect for the national will, which was the essence of democracy in the 1960s, had been taken as a basis, we would not have in our memories the shame of a country that executed its Prime Minister and ministers. If the 1970s had passed with democratic maturity, our young people would not have been executed, workers would not have been targeted with the January 24 Decisions, or tank tracks would have crushed politicians and the public on September 12. If "Nevroz", a people's holiday in 1992, had been accepted as a common value rather than a threat, dark structures might not have collapsed like a nightmare in our 90s. If democratic norms, not tutelage, had been valid in this country in 2007, the coup attempt called "367" would not have taken place in the dark pages of our history. When history writes about the July 15 coup attempt, if our answer had been more democracy, justice and law, rather than the July 20 declaration of state of emergency, this country would have been a great hope for all the people of the Middle East. Today, we would be talking about hopes, the budget of the workers and the oppressed, not the risks and threats.

When the calendar turned to November 4, 2016, if the Selahattins and Figens had not been taken from their homes and trustees had not been appointed to the municipalities, democratic politics would not have been hit. Lenses from 2025
When we turn it into bitter memories of the past, every anti-democratic practice returned to the citizens of this country as poverty, tears, pain and sorrow. If the republic met with democracy in Türkiye, every citizen living in these lands would have a completely different story.
Dear President, esteemed MPs; There is a historical rupture that consumed all the energy of our country; caused huge social and economic costs. We have left a century behind with the Kurdish rebellions that started after 1923 and the suppression of these rebellions. While talking about the Kurdish issue, we must first determine this: The most fundamental mistake of those who have governed this country for a century is that they misdiagnosed the Kurdish issue. When his diagnosis was wrong, the ways to eliminate the Kurdish problem were also wrong. Knots have been tied over the Kurdish issue for years, and this knot has almost turned into a Gordian knot. For years, this issue has been squeezed into the bracket of terrorism, "underdevelopment." It was said, "A handful of people who were deceived and deceived." It was said. However, the Kurdish issue is not a problem of underdevelopment, the Kurdish issue is not the problem of a deceived or deceived community, the Kurdish issue is not a problem of terrorism at all; It is a matter of equal citizenship, a matter of democratic rights, a matter of existence, but most importantly, the Kurdish issue is a matter of law. Recognizing Kurdish law is also possible for a unitary state. What does "unitary state" mean, a denial of the state?
nor is it the denial of the Kurds. Kurds' defense of their rights as equal citizens is not a risk for the unitary state, but rather an assurance. The Kurdish issue is not a topic to be sacrificed to the noise of daily politics, it is a historical phenomenon in which lives are lost and hearths are extinguished. The Kurdish issue has been managed with a no-nonsense mentality for years and the result has always been disappointment. We now say that we should establish peace politics with a solution mind.
Esteemed MPs, your road map is shaped accordingly from which perspective you look at history. Unfortunately, some truths were ignored in the historical horizon that the state looked at. İsmail Suphi Soysallıoğlu, the spokesman of the commission that prepared the 1921 Constitution, suggested steps prioritizing localism in the minutes of the First Assembly. Similarly, it is necessary to establish the scales of justice in the Mustafa Kemal issue. While Mustafa Kemal in 1920, 1921 and 1922 is consigned to the dusty shelves of history, Atatürk after 1923 is kept at the forefront of the official narrative. Mustafa Kemal, who accepted local democracy while writing the 1921 Constitution, should not be erased from history, but should be taken down from the dusty shelves of history. We must now leave aside the official narrative that erases this truth and focus on the truth. The founding horizon of 1921, which prioritized locality, stands before us today as a forgotten possibility. Exactly one hundred and six years ago, in the final declaration of the Erzurum Congress, the following was written with reference to the Turkish-Kurdish partnership:
"It accepts full partnership in happiness and disaster and aims at the same goal about the future." In other words, at the foundation stage, the idea of ​​a common homeland and the idea of ​​law stand side by side, but then the law part is forgotten.

The Republic is declared after a liberation supported by the Kurds, but Kurdish law is pushed out of the founding texts. In his group speech dated November 18, 2025, Mr. Devlet Bahçeli made a very important observation about the founding period of the republic: "The preparation and fermentation period was carried out through congresses, that is, through democratic means." Actually, it indicates that we are taking more democracy as a basis today. Just as the deep crisis of the Ottoman Empire could be overcome that day by congresses and the Parliament, that is, by the pluralistic representation of the will of the people, the solution to the regional threats and multiple internal crises that Türkiye faces today will only be possible with democracy. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
When we look at history honestly, we see that one of the important reasons for the heavy defeat in the Balkans was the unity that could not be achieved in Anatolia. If the Kurds are in danger, the Turks are also in danger. We are two people whose destinies are intertwined. The secret of winning the War of Independence is the unity achieved in Anatolia. As the Soviet Union collapsed in the early 1990s, great geopolitical opportunities arose for Türkiye.
The mentality that insists on not solving the Kurdish issue has spent all its energy. The gangs and dark structures surrounding the state squandered these opportunities. If the Kurdish issue had been resolved, the 90s could have turned into a period of tremendous democracy and leap for Türkiye. We have spent a century with internal tensions, now we have to look at the past and learn lessons. Mehmet Akif Ersoy, the poet of the National Anthem, said, "They describe history as 'repeating itself'/Would it repeat itself if any lessons were taken?" says. We invite everyone to learn the right lesson from history and to "vifakı kadime", that is, eternal and ancient brotherhood. (Applause from the DEM PARTY ranks) We must all step into the new century together, where we will face dangers together but also experience happiness together.
Mr. President, dear MPs; At the beginning of the century, all that remained of the world state was Anatolia. This massive contraction sowed the seeds of historical fears that have never subsided among the Turks. Among the Kurds, the insecurity and suspicion of being left in the lurch remains, but there is another truth; We have no choice but each other, we are two walls of a room in a house facing each other, we have a strong memory that will nourish inner peace. This geography is a geography where not only states and empires but also wisdom, mercy and justice were born, lived and spread for centuries. Isn't that why a Mevlevi hymn, a Turkish bozlak,
We share the same feeling when we hear a Kurdish "."(*), an Armenian lament, or a Laz bagpipe. "."(*) bards were nourished by this ancient culture, wise men and dervishes drank from the same spring. Kurdish poet Cigerxwin describes every wealth as the rushing of rivers in the line "."(*). Our feeling is Cigerxwin's feeling. (Applause from the DEM PARTY rows) Great writer Yaşar Kemal "We are two wings of a bird, if one of them is broken, we cannot fly." He stated that one of us cannot find ourselves without the other. The path to peace and brotherhood is hidden in the words of Yunus Emre. What does Yunus say: "Let's get to know each other. Let's make the job easier."

"Growing peace and unity is possible through meeting and dialogue; we know that the words 'talk' and 'neighbor' are derived from the verb 'to settle', that is, to settle somewhere. 'Talk and neighborliness, for Kurds and Turks, means standing on the same branch, looking at the same view together. When we forget this, differences grow, when we remember this, togetherness grows.
Dear MPs, we have witnessed great pain and destruction during the conflict that lasted for half a century. There were millions who lost, but unfortunately there were also winners. There was also a section of capital and bureaucracy that fed on conflict, and some political focuses that fed on the politics of hostility. This minority group is the leader of the state, politics, media and economy.
It seeped everywhere. What we call "impact mechanics" happens precisely through these dynamics that permeate everywhere. Mr. Öcalan's "coup mechanics" statement has been greatly distorted. Coup mechanics is the organization and activation of historical fears and reflexes by many seemingly disparate circles. We can only overcome the coup mechanics by strengthening the will for peace and determination for a solution. (Applause from DEM PARTY rows) Italian philosopher Gramsci says: "The old world is dying, the new one cannot be born. Monsters are emerging in this twilight." This is exactly the threshold we are in today. Today, we must be more determined than ever against those who prevent the birth of peace, that is, those who put their own interests before the common good, and we must be able to give birth to a new day together.
Our valuable people watching us on their screens, the main issue today is for the government to switch to a fair and democratic understanding of government. Trying to manage 86 million citizens, 100 thousands of schools, thousands of public institutions and millions of people from the center is now both impossible and unhealthy. The requirement of democratic mind is to strengthen local governments, the spirit of the time is local democracy. You cannot solve the problem in the neighborhood from a single table in Ankara. A Türkiye that cannot adopt law and democracy can resolve neither administrative, ethnic nor religious tensions. The problem of a village in the Hemşin Plateau on the banks of the Tigris can only be resolved together with that village.
A locality can solve it. Local democracy is the glue that holds the country together. We want local governments to be governed through representatives elected by the people, not through trustee practices. Strengthening local governments is the right of the citizens of Manisa as well as the citizens of Diyarbakır. These steps are important steps that will reinforce not only the law of the Kurds, but also the legitimacy of the republic within the law.
Dear friends, when discussing the last forty years of the Kurdish issue, there is a fact that everyone knows but often avoids discussing out loud: Mr. Öcalan is an actor that cannot be ignored in the solution of this issue. (Applause from the DEM PARTY ranks) We all know that he has made various ceasefire and negotiation calls at different periods since 1993. The solution discussions that started on the occasion of the opening of the Parliament on October 1, 2024, showed us once again that this issue can be solved by talking, not by silencing. Every actor that can have an impact on ending the conflict must be present within the framework of the law, on a transparent and democratic basis of negotiation.

There is nothing more natural than Öcalan, who has determined the course of this process, speaking out for peace. The way to achieve this is to ensure living, communication and working conditions. At this very point, Mr. Öcalan said, "We want to include the Kurds in the republic through the legal gate." We must state that the determination is very important and critical. republic just
It was not a regime change declared in 1923, a republic was declared but left incomplete. Today, we have to complete that deficiency, we can complete this by adding the Kurdish phenomenon to the legality of the republic. So, can this deficiency be completed by just including the Kurds? Of course not. In the 2nd century of our Republic, we desire a new and democratic Türkiye where excluded groups come to the center. When the 2nd century of the Republic is crowned with democracy, the sense of belonging and loyalty of all segments to the country increases.
So, what can we do to ensure the internal integrity of the country? We can establish a trans-identity legal language in the Constitution that strengthens the principle of equal citizenship, and we can guarantee education in the mother tongue and access to public services as universal rights. (Applause from the DEM PARTY benches) We can strengthen social harmony with mutual trust and determination by passing democratic integration laws. With the peace law, we can resolve the ethnic, religious and class tensions in society on the basis of equality and freedom. We can ensure the full implementation of the decisions of the ECHR and the Constitutional Court and bring the criminal and execution legislation to democratic standards.
While talking about today, let's open the door of history and take a look at the past. In the 1500s, the Ottoman ruler sent a white paper with his seal on the bottom along with a note to the Kurds. The note reads exactly as follows: "Write whatever you wish, it has the value of law."
This narrative is very important as it shows how well a government knows and values ​​the Kurds. What we need today is someone who hands out the white paper not only to the Kurds, but to all citizens, saying "Let's create the law of this country together." It is the democratic republic mind that says. It is now necessary to put an end to the dual understanding of law that has accompanied the century-old republic and establish peace with holistic law.
Dear friends, a hundred years ago, imperialist powers divided this geography with a ruler. The main reason for the almost endless tensions, genocides, migrations and rebellions in the Middle East today is the interventions of imperialist powers a hundred years ago; They drew not only the borders but also our destinies. We must now get out of this vortex, we must make the borders in the Middle East meaningless without changing them. We can hold the key to regional peace by increasing social interaction, ensuring economic integration and political stability, because the people of this region are tired of fighting; Turks, Kurds, Arabs and Persians now want to live together in prosperity and mutual respect. The people of the region were tired of being driven from front to front and mourning the losses. The tragedy experienced in Palestine is the deepest wound of this fatigue engraved on the conscience. The oppression suffered by the Palestinian people must end forever. The only way to throw Sykes-Picot into the dustbin of history is to implement unifying policies in the region.

Right here in the Middle East
In Syria, which has become a bleeding country, we must contribute to unifying models instead of divisive models. The biggest lesson to be learned from Syria is that dialogue, not military options, is the solution. We believe that a decentralized management approach in Syria, where local governments are strong and respects differences, is the most appropriate solution. (Applause from the DEM PARTY benches) All the people there are our brothers. Türkiye should be a reliable partner for all the people there. Democratic Middle East unity can be built with the common will of peoples and beliefs. Türkiye can play a leading role in this process with its republican experience, geographical location and Kurdish-Turkish common historical heritage. Türkiye will benefit if it establishes a regional strategy that thinks together with the Kurds. In this sense, we should be able to look at Northern and Eastern Syria through a democratic dialogue window, not through a security lens. Of course, such a historical transformation requires the contribution and courage of all political actors. That's why I want to make a sincere call to all segments from here, first of all I call out to the executive power: As the power in power, the biggest responsibility lies on your shoulders. Saving the state is possible not only in war but also in peace. Today, you are at the threshold of hundreds of years of history. Please show a little more determination, sincerity and courage. I also call out to those who insist on the securityist line: Of course, every fight will end in peace one day.
Every conflict ultimately ends with talking, dialogue and negotiation; Even the most experienced members of the armed forces say, "This job does not end with weapons." they say. What is expected from you is not to hinder peace efforts, but on the contrary, to facilitate them. Non-conflict is an atmosphere where everyone wins. Can there be greater national security than peace? I also call out to the main opposition circles: We have to present peace to this country with a principled stance, there is no more sacred political duty before us.
An opposition that does not favor a solution to the Kurdish issue and postpones the solution to another spring cannot be an alternative to Türkiye, and the solution of this problem cannot be left only to the state and the government. I also call out to my centre-right, undecided and nationalist brothers: It is possible to achieve unity in this country without alienating any people and giving them their rights. You are also responsible for establishing a union based on consent, not a union based on force. "Dialogue first, people first." You should be able to say. (Applause from the DEM PARTY benches) I call out to all colors of Türkiye: Come, let's remove prejudices; I say, let's strengthen and democratize the country by reviving the law of brotherhood on the basis of equality. Türkiye is the country of all of us. I would like to state once again that we, as the DEM PARTY, will do our best to help the state confront the mistakes made in the past and implement a more inclusive and encompassing policy. We want democratic rights and freedoms for everyone, we want democratic space
We are ready to come together with everyone to expand it.

The people of this country actually do not want much; He wants to live in a Türkiye where he breathes freely with his own identity and beliefs, where there is no poverty, where knocking on the door at dawn calls for trust, not fear. From this rostrum, I call and invite the Assembly to the mind of courage instead of the inertia of fear, and to the broad horizon of sincerity, not the narrow scales of the mind. We will establish peace with the mortar of courage and the will of 86 million. We are ready to be the carrier and builder of democratic Türkiye. In his last group speech, President Erdoğan said, "We will succeed this time." he said. We express it clearly here: Yes, we want to succeed this time, we will succeed.
I offer my respect and gratitude to all of you. (Applause from DEM PARTY, AK PARTY, MHP and YENİ YOL rows)

records.englishTranslation: "I swear on my honor and dignity before the great Turkish nation that I will protect the existence and independence of the state, the indivisible integrity of the homeland and the nation, the unconditional sovereignty of the nation; that I will abide by the rule of law, the democratic and secular Republic, and Ataturk's principles and reforms; that I will not deviate from the ideal of society's peace and welfare, that everyone should enjoy human rights and fundamental freedoms within the understanding of national solidarity and justice, and from loyalty to the Constitution."

records.originalTurkish:
TUNCER BAKIRHAN (Siirt) - Sayın Başkan, sayın milletvekilleri; hepinizi selamlıyorum. Partim adına kanun teklifinin 11'inci maddesi üzerine söz almış bulunmaktayım.

Başta şunu belirtmek gerekiyor ki özellikle Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sistemine geçişle birlikte Mecliste geçen her iki kanunundan biri torba yasa olmuştur. Deyim yerinde ise Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sistemi, torba yasa hükûmet sistemi hâlini almıştır.

Meclisin 28'inci Dönemine yine bir torba yasayla başlıyoruz. Örneğin 30 maddelik bir kanun çıkarıyorsunuz ve birbirinden farklı onlarca konuyu aynı kanun içerisinde düzenliyorsunuz, bu durum yasama bütünlüğünü bozarak milletvekillerinin kanunu denetleme fırsatını da ortadan kaldırıyor. Torba yasalar yasama etiğini ayaklar altına alıyor, bu durum aynı zamanda hâkimlerin, savcıların, hukukçuların ve bürokratların torba yasalarla değişen kanunları takip edebilmesini de ortadan kaldırıyor.

Madde 11'le Ocak 2023 tarihinden itibaren geçerli olmak üzere Hazine ve Maliye Bakanı ve Cumhurbaşkanının yüzde beşer olan borç artırım tutarı 3 katına çıkarılmaktadır. Bu düzenlemeyle Meclisin bütçe yapma yetkisi hiçe sayılarak bütçenin içine tek kişinin iradesiyle harcanacak bir başka bütçe daha yerleştirilmiş ve Meclisin bütçe hakkı gasbedilmiş oluyor. Yasama organının kamu harcamalarının çeşit ve miktarı ile kaynağını önceden belirleme ve onaylama yetkisi bütçe hakkının bir gereğidir. Bütçe kanunları yasama organı tarafından yürütme organına yıllık olarak kamu gelirlerinin toplanması ve giderlerinin yapılması için yetki ve izin verilmesini düzenleyen kanunlardır. Verilen bu izin ve yetki özünde yasamanın halktan aldığı bütçe hakkının gereğidir. Anayasa'nın 161'inci maddesinin yedinci fıkrasında ise "Merkezî yönetim bütçesiyle verilen ödenek, harcanabilecek tutarın sınırını gösterir." deniyor. Anayasa diyor ki: "Harcanabilecek tutarın Cumhurbaşkanlığı kararnamesiyle aşılabileceğine dair bütçe kanununa hüküm konulamaz." Ama iç borçlanma olunca bu Hükûmet yasa falan dinlemiyor "Bütçe kanununa ilişkin Cumhurbaşkanına yetki verilemez." demiyor.

Bu konuda daha önceden verilmiş bir iptal kararı mevcut. Şimdi, bu amir hüküm yok sayılarak ve yine bir torba kanun içerisine net borç kullanım tutarının artırılmasıyla ilgili olarak bu maddeyi koyuyorsunuz. Buradan sormak istiyoruz: Anayasa'da ilgili maddeler bu kadar açıkken ve daha önce bu konuda verilmiş iptal kararı varken 11'inci maddeyi bu teklifin içerisine koyma amacınız nedir? Bu madde yasalaşırsa ödenek üstü harcama yapılması kaçınılmaz olur. Meclis bu maddenin onaylanmasına izin verirse Meclisin bütçe yapmasının da bir anlamı kalmaz.

Değerli arkadaşlar, görünen o ki el parasıyla saadet döneminin sonuna geliyoruz. İktidarı döneminde dış borcu yönetilemez ve sürdürülemez düzeylere taşıyan AK PARTİ, nesiller boyu ödemek zorunda kalacağımız büyük bir borç mirası bırakmaktadır.

Sonuç olarak, borçlanma talep eden bu düzenlemenin, borçlanma limitine ilişkin muğlaklık içermesi ve buna ilişkin farklı yorumlara açık olması, halkın sırtına ek, büyük maddi yükler getirmesi ve bütçe hakkını gasbetmesi dolayısıyla çıkarılması gerekmektedir.
records.englishTranslation:
TUNCER BAKIRHAN (Siirt) - Mr. President, dear members of parliament; I greet you all. I have taken the floor on behalf of my party on the 11th article of the bill.

First of all, it should be noted that, especially with the transition to the Presidential government system, one of every two laws passed in the Parliament became an omnibus law. So to speak, the Presidential system of government has become an omnibus law system of government.

We are starting the 28th Term of the Parliament with another omnibus bill. For example, you enact a law with 30 articles and regulate dozens of different issues within the same law, which disrupts the integrity of the legislature and eliminates the opportunity for MPs to audit the law. Omnibus laws trample on legislative ethics, and this also eliminates the ability of judges, prosecutors, lawyers and bureaucrats to follow the laws changed by omnibus laws.

With Article 11, the debt increase amount of five percent each of the Minister of Treasury and Finance and the President is tripled, effective as of January 2023. With this regulation, the Parliament's budget-making authority is disregarded, another budget to be spent by a single person is placed in the budget, and the Parliament's budget right is usurped. The power of the legislative body to predetermine and approve the type, amount and source of public expenditures is a requirement of the budget right. Budget laws are laws that regulate the authority and permission given by the legislative body to the executive body for the annual collection of public revenues and expenditures. This permission and authority given is essentially a requirement of the budget right that the legislature receives from the public. In the seventh paragraph of Article 161 of the Constitution, "The appropriation provided by the central government budget indicates the limit of the amount that can be spent." It is said. The Constitution says: "No provision can be included in the budget law stating that the amount that can be spent can be exceeded by a Presidential decree." But when there is domestic debt, this government does not listen to the law and says, "The President cannot be given authority regarding the budget law." It doesn't say.

There is a previous cancellation decision on this issue. Now, you are ignoring this mandatory provision and putting this article into an omnibus law regarding increasing the net debt utilization amount. We would like to ask here: What is your purpose in including Article 11 in this proposal, when the relevant articles are so clear in the Constitution and there has been a previous annulment decision on this issue? If this article becomes law, spending beyond the appropriations will be inevitable. If the Parliament allows this article to be approved, there will be no point in the Parliament making a budget.

Dear friends, it seems that we are coming to the end of the era of financial happiness. AK PARTY, which increased the foreign debt to unmanageable and unsustainable levels during its rule, leaves a legacy of great debt that we will have to pay for generations.

As a result, this regulation requiring borrowing should be removed because it contains ambiguity regarding the borrowing limit and is open to different interpretations, brings additional, large financial burdens on the public and usurps the budget right.
